item.title: As workers worry about AI, Nvidia’s Jensen Huang says AI is ‘creating an enormous number of jobs’
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The Nvidia CEO seems to feel that claims of AI's job-killing potential have been greatly exaggerated.

item.title: OpenAI’s cozy partner Cerebras is on track for a blockbuster IPO
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: AI chip maker Cerebras is heading for a blockbuster IPO that could value it at $26.6 billion or more. Its relationship with OpenAI is deep and rich.

item.title: Image AI models now drive app growth, beating chatbot upgrades
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Appfigures finds visual model launches generate 6.5x more downloads — but most don’t convert that spike into revenue.

item.title: Elon Musk’s only AI expert witness at the OpenAI trial fears an AGI arms race
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Stuart Russell is a long-time AI researcher who thinks governments need to restrain frontier labs.

item.title: Sierra raises $950M as the race to own enterprise AI gets serious
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The raise gives Sierra more than $1 billion to work with — capital the company says it will use to become the "global standard" for AI-powered customer experiences.

item.title: Elon Musk sent ominous texts to Greg Brockman, Sam Altman after asking for a settlement, OpenAI claims
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort, Tim Fernholz
item.description: Musk texted OpenAI's president and co-founder saying that he and CEO Sam Altman "will be the most hated men in America" if OpenAI doesn't settle the suit.

item.title: Anthropic and OpenAI are both launching joint ventures for enterprise AI services
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Both Anthropic and OpenAI have partnered with asset managers to more aggressively market their enterprise AI products.

item.title: 5 days only: Bring a partner or colleague and get 50% off a second TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: The BOGO offer is live. For a limited time, buy one pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 and get 50% off a second of the same ticket type. Offer ends this Friday, May 8. Save here.

item.title: DoorDash adds AI tools to speed up merchant onboarding, edit photos of dishes
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: DoorDash on Monday added new AI-powered tools that let merchants speed up onboarding, edit photos to make dishes look better, and create new websites from existing content.

item.title: ‘This is fine’ creator says AI startup stole his art
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The ad comes from Artisan, the AI startup behind billboards urging businesses to "stop hiring humans."

item.title: In Harvard study, AI offered more accurate emergency room diagnoses than two human doctors
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: A new study examines how large language models perform in a variety of medical contexts, including real emergency room cases — where at least one model seemed to be more accurate than human doctors.

item.title: AI-generated actors and scripts are now ineligible for Oscars
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.category: academy of motion picture arts and sciences
item.description: The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences said that only performances “credited in the film’s legal billing and demonstrably performed by humans with their consent” will be eligible for Academy Awards.

item.title: The best AI dictation apps, tested and ranked
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: AI-powered dictation apps are useful for replying to emails, taking notes, and even coding through your voice

item.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: At TechCrunch's sold-out StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, we covered a lot of ground in a short time, beginning with the question everyone in the industry is asking right now: In a world where rival Cursor is reportedly in talks to be acquired by SpaceX for $60 billion, is Replit also bound to sell?

item.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Meta bought humanoid startup Assured Robot Intelligence to beef up its AI models for robots, the company said.

item.title: Did you know you can’t steal a charity? Don’t worry. Elon Musk will remind you.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets are surfacing in court, and there are plenty more witnesses to come. Musk’s argument against OpenAI? By converting the company to a for-profit model, Sam Altman betrayed the “nonprofit for the […]

item.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

item.title: Musk v. Altman is just getting started
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets are surfacing in court, and there are plenty more witnesses to come. Musk’s argument against OpenAI? By converting the company to a for-profit model, Sam Altman betrayed the “nonprofit for the […]

item.title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Users in India are embracing ChatGPT Images 2.0 for creative, personal visuals — from avatars to cinematic portraits.

item.title: Sources: Anthropic potential $900B+ valuation round could happen within 2 weeks
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Anthropic is asking investors to submit allocations for the AI company’s latest fundraise within the next 48 hours, according to sources familiar with the matter.
